explanation: Noninflammatory bilateral parotid enlargement (sialadenosis/sialadenosis-like patterns) is associated with acinar hypertrophy on histology. Inflammatory sialadenitis more often shows infiltrate and later acinar atrophy; among the listed options, acinar hypertrophy is the keyed biopsy finding for that swelling pattern.

Median rhomboid glossitis is a form of chronic erythematous candidiasis of the midline posterior dorsal tongue and is strongly associated with Candida, often with kissing contact against the palate under a complete denture (chronic atrophic candidiasis). Staph/S. mutans are not the primary drivers.

After a needlestick from an HIV-positive source, immediate first aid (wash) is followed by urgent occupational assessment and post-exposure prophylaxis started as soon as possible (ideally within hours), not delayed for source testing alone. PEP is the critical next clinical step among the options.

In RPD design the mandibular minor connector joins the major connector at a rigid butt-type junction (right-angle/butt joint), not a ball-and-socket or acrylic ‘joint.’ This provides rigidity for force transmission without a movable articulation.

Wits appraisal measures the linear distance between projections of points A and B onto the functional occlusal plane (not the anatomic occlusal plane). It assesses sagittal jaw discrepancy independent of cranial-base angle variation.

Dental unit waterline biofilm thrives where water sits unused. Preventing stagnation (regular flushing, anti-retraction, chemical treatment, never leaving lines idle without management) is the key control measure against biofilm accumulation.

Cross-infection is transmission of infectious agents between patients, staff, or environment during healthcare—including colleague-to-colleague spread via contaminated instruments, surfaces, or aerosols. That definition matches the stem.

In one-stage (non-submerged) implant protocols a transmucosal healing abutment (often dome-shaped) is placed at surgery so soft tissue heals around an open emergence profile. It shapes peri-implant mucosa until the definitive abutment/crown.

Night sweats plus hemoptysis/blood-tinged sputum are classic red flags for pulmonary tuberculosis (or other serious lung disease). TB is the working diagnosis to suspect among the listed options until proven otherwise.

Metal impression trays are heat-stable; after cleaning, thermal processing (washer-disinfector/hot-water thermal disinfection pathway and/or autoclave as protocol allows) is preferred. Glutaraldehyde 10% for 10 minutes is not standard tray practice; brush-only is incomplete.

Conventional MTA formulations include bismuth oxide as the radiopacifier so the material is visible on radiographs. Calcium silicate is the hydraulic base; NaOCl is an irrigant, not an MTA radiopacity additive.

On electronic apex locators, the red/over zone indicates the file tip has passed the apical foramen (beyond the apex). Green/zero typically marks the apical constriction; short readings stay coronal to that landmark.

Osteoid osteoma classically causes night pain that is dramatically relieved by NSAIDs/aspirin (prostaglandin-mediated). Fibroma and other listed distractors do not share that aspirin-responsive pain pattern.

Dependent pitting edema of the lower legs in a patient with cardiac history indicates right-sided/congestive heart failure with fluid retention. Infective endocarditis and hypertension alone do not define that dependent edema sign.

For fixed orthodontic appliances, the Charter (or modified Charter) brushing method angles bristles toward the occlusal/incisal aspect of brackets and uses vibratory strokes to clean under wires and around hardware better than Bass alone.

Immediate latex rubber-dam allergy is type I (IgE-mediated) hypersensitivity—urticaria, bronchospasm, anaphylaxis. Delayed contact dermatitis from rubber chemicals is type IV; the acute latex protein reaction board hinge is type I.

Bald red (atrophic) tongue with angular cheilitis/crusting at the commissures suggests nutritional deficiency (iron, B12, folate) or anemia-related glossitis. Order CBC (and related labs) rather than assuming pure local infection alone.
Primary jaw closers are masseter, temporalis, and medial pterygoid. Digastric (with lateral pterygoid and suprahyoids) depresses/opens the mandible. Among listed muscles, digastric is the depressor.

A short-term interim/temporary RPD after a single extraction is usually acrylic-based without cast rests; rests are features of definitive cast frameworks. No rests is appropriate for a provisional flipper-style prosthesis until definitive care.

Bilateral exophthalmos with systemic signs including hypertension/thyrotoxic features points to Graves disease (autoimmune hyperthyroidism with orbitopathy). Hashimoto/hypothyroidism lack this classic proptosis pattern; tumor is usually unilateral.

Vertical root fracture is confirmed definitively by direct visualization (surgical exploration/flap or extraction inspection). CBCT and multiple PAs can support suspicion but do not always prove VRF; exploratory surgery is the gold-standard confirmation.

Large open interproximal contacts are cleaned most effectively with an interdental brush sized to the embrasure. Standard floss and superfloss work better in tight contacts; a conventional toothbrush alone does not clean mid-embrasure plaque well.

Dentin hypersensitivity with gingival recession localizes to exposed cervical root dentin (open tubules near the CEJ), not mid-root bulk or crown enamel. Cervical root is the classic site of recession-related sensitivity.

Carisolv is a chemomechanical caries-removal system (sodium hypochlorite-based gel with amino acids) used with hand instruments to soften and excavate carious dentin selectively without rotary cutting as the primary method.

Significant renal impairment is a classic caution for fluoride exposure because reduced renal clearance raises systemic fluoride load. Among listed organ systems, renal disease is the keyed reason to avoid/limit fluoride varnish or systemic fluoride decisions per protocol.

Abruptly cutting off a patient’s personal narrative solely to demand the chief complaint violates treating the patient as a person (respect, listening, person-centered care). It is not primarily a privacy, consent, or disinfection issue.

Silver points corrode in tissue fluids; corrosion products are toxic and associated with inflammation and treatment failure. Corrosion-related toxicity is the classic disadvantage that led to abandonment of silver points.

Transillumination (fiber-optic light) helps visualize crack lines as they block light transmission. Bite-on-release pain supports cracked-tooth syndrome clinically, but among options, transillumination is the direct confirmatory chairside optical test for a crack.

Mild bilateral tuberosity undercuts that aid denture retention are often managed by relieving the denture base rather than surgically removing both undercuts. Aggressive bilateral reduction is reserved when insertion path or trauma makes undercuts harmful.

Electrosurgery/electrocautery can generate electromagnetic interference that may affect pacemakers and trigger arrhythmia. Intraoral films, panoramic imaging, and high-speed handpieces are not the classic pacemaker EMI culprits in this vignette.
